text: When you do a slash review command, on top of asking if you want to do it in a new chat, if you choose to do it in a new chat, it should also ask what model you want to use from your currently scoped models and present them.

We need to make it more clear that you have to switch posture from plan mode to act mode to write files because some models keep accidentally writing files before trying to switch posture first, which is a waste.

we need to update the postures or rather the tools that are available in the review and debug postures to actually be the same as plan because the idea is for those three modes to essentially be the same in terms of what they're capable of doing. Only navigating and understanding the repository and potentially writing files to specific locations like a review or a plan. and essentially only act should be the thing that is allowed to actually manipulate the code base.

We need a stash and pop system for user prompts that works nearly identical to how a good portion of the ideas feature works in terms of storing it in a JSON L document inserting it back into the in user's input box area only diffly difference would be when it's rendering the list to select from you know which stashed things you want to restore when you restore one it, removes it from the list.

And if you want to put it back in the list, you would have to stash again. So that way the list isn't ever just constantly full of things. and specifically the way it would work as well is stash needs to be based on a key binding because you're not in while you're in the middle of typing a prompt going to be able to do like a slash command or something.

So it needs to be based on a key binding. And it should then notify too that it was stashed.

We need to figure out why every model except ChatGPT, when it switches posture from plan to act, can't seem to discover the fact that it has a bash tool. GLM, Mini Max M3, Kimmy all had the same problems
